name: Kingdom of Croatia (Habsburg)

colors.symbolism: the Kingdom of Slavonia and the Croatian chequy
the Kingdom of Slavonia and Dalmatia; the third pan-Slavic colour

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of red, white and blue with the Croatian red-and-white chequy (sahovnica) shield in the centre.
history: The red-white-blue Croatian tricolour was first raised in 1848 during the Croatian national revival, at the installation of Ban Josip Jelacic, within the Habsburg Monarchy. The Kingdom of Croatia was a Habsburg crown land from 1527 until 1868, when it was merged with Slavonia to form the autonomous Kingdom of Croatia-Slavonia; the tricolour continued as the Croatian flag thereafter and is the direct ancestor of the modern Croatian flag.

features.symbolism: the historic colours of the kingdoms of Croatia, Slavonia and Dalmatia; the pan-Slavic tricolour
the red-and-white chequered shield of Croatia, in use since at least the late 15th century
